# AgentStandard — Package Category Pipeline

**Generated by Scout** | 2026-03-03

> Existing 10 categories excluded. This document covers the next 20 candidates.

---

## Category Table

| # | Category Name | Target Operator | Core Value | Key Skills / Tools | Tier | Market Size |
|---|---------------|-----------------|------------|--------------------|------|-------------|
| 11 | **Health Coach** | Individuals tracking fitness, chronic conditions, or weight loss | Replaces fragmented app ecosystem by holding longitudinal health context across workouts, nutrition, labs, and goals — then proactively nudging behaviour | MyFitnessPal web scrape, Cronometer API, Fitbit/Garmin export, web_search (nutrition/studies), memory | Beginner | Mass market |
| 12 | **Real Estate Scout** | First-time buyers, property investors, BTL landlords | Monitors listings across portals, tracks price history, flags value anomalies, and builds a persistent shortlist with comparative analysis | Rightmove/Zoopla/Zillow web scrape, Google Maps, mortgage calc tools, memory | Intermediate | Large |
| 13 | **Job Hunt Ops** | Active job seekers, career changers, recent grads | Tracks applications, tailors CVs per role, researches companies before interviews, and manages follow-up cadence so nothing slips | LinkedIn scrape, web_search, doc editor, memory/calendar, email drafting | Intermediate | Large |
| 14 | **Research Librarian** | Students, academics, indie researchers, curious professionals | Builds a living knowledge base from papers, articles, and books — synthesises themes, surfaces contradictions, and answers cross-source questions | web_fetch, arxiv/PubMed scrape, PDF reader, memory, Zotero-compatible export | Intermediate | Medium |
| 15 | **Sales Prospector** | SMB founders, freelancers, B2B sales reps without CRM support | Researches leads from public signals (LinkedIn, news, job posts), builds tailored outreach context, and tracks prospect status without enterprise CRM | LinkedIn/web scrape, web_search, email drafting, memory, HubSpot free API | Intermediate | Large |
| 16 | **Travel Planner** | Frequent travellers, digital nomads, family holiday organisers | Builds and manages trip itineraries end-to-end — flights, accommodation, activities, visas, local intel — and updates plans proactively as dates approach | Skyscanner/Google Flights web scrape, Google Maps, weather skill, memory, calendar | Beginner | Mass market |
| 17 | **Social Listener** | Brand managers, indie founders, podcasters, niche community builders | Monitors mentions, sentiment, and trending topics across Reddit, X, and HN — then flags opportunities and threats in real time | Reddit API, web_search, X scrape, memory, notification hooks | Intermediate | Medium |
| 18 | **Startup Advisor** | Solo founders, pre-seed teams, indie hackers building 0→1 | Acts as a persistent strategic sounding board — tracks build progress, challenges decisions, benchmarks against comparable companies, and flags dead ends | web_search, web_fetch, memory, Crunchbase public data, doc drafting | Advanced | Medium |
| 19 | **Personal Finance** | Salaried individuals, young professionals, people living paycheque to paycheque | Tracks spending patterns from bank exports, spots budget drift, models savings scenarios, and delivers weekly financial health summaries | CSV/PDF parser, web_search (rates/deals), memory, spreadsheet tools | Beginner | Mass market |
| 20 | **PR & Outreach** | Indie founders, authors, podcasters, small agency owners | Finds relevant journalists/podcasters, drafts personalised pitches, tracks send history, and follows up without nagging — like having a PR agent on retainer | web_search, web_fetch, email drafting, memory, media database scraping | Intermediate | Medium |
| 21 | **Learning Path** | Career switchers, autodidacts, professionals upskilling in new domains | Builds a structured, adaptive curriculum from free resources, tracks progress, quizzes the user, and adjusts pacing based on retained knowledge | web_search, YouTube scrape, memory, flashcard generation, progress tracking | Beginner | Large |
| 22 | **Crypto Desk** | Retail crypto traders, DeFi participants, on-chain degens | Monitors wallet activity, tracks protocol TVL/yield shifts, summarises on-chain signals, and flags position risks without requiring exchange API keys | CoinGecko API, Etherscan API (free), DeFiLlama API, web_search, memory | Advanced | Medium |
| 23 | **Grant Hunter** | Academics, charities, social enterprises, arts organisations, startups | Continuously scans grant databases for eligible opportunities, tracks deadlines, and drafts application sections — turning grant-hunting from a job into a queue | web_fetch (gov/foundation portals), web_search, doc drafting, memory, calendar | Intermediate | Medium |
| 24 | **Parenting Ops** | Parents of young children, homeschoolers, special needs families | Tracks developmental milestones, manages appointment schedules, researches age-appropriate activities, and surfaces relevant health guidance when needed | web_search, calendar, memory, NHS/CDC public data fetch, doc templates | Beginner | Large |
| 25 | **Podcast Producer** | Solo podcasters, interview hosts, content studios without production staff | Handles pre-interview research, generates episode briefs and question sets, drafts show notes, timestamps, and social clips post-recording | web_fetch, web_search, doc editor, memory, YouTube/transcript API | Intermediate | Medium |
| 26 | **Competitive Intel** | Product managers, strategy teams, startup founders, analysts | Tracks competitor product changes, pricing updates, job postings (as a signal), and press — and synthesises weekly battle cards | web_scrape, web_search, memory, doc drafting, notification hooks | Advanced | Medium |
| 27 | **Tax Prep Aide** | Freelancers, contractors, landlords, side-hustlers with complex returns | Organises receipts, categorises expenses, estimates tax liability, tracks allowable deductions, and flags anomalies before submission — without touching returns itself | PDF/CSV parser, HMRC/IRS public guidance fetch, memory, spreadsheet tools | Intermediate | Large |
| 28 | **Event Coordinator** | Corporate PA teams, community organisers, wedding/event planners at SMB scale | Manages vendor shortlists, tracks RSVPs, drafts run-of-show documents, sends reminders, and keeps budget vs actuals updated across the lifecycle | Calendar, email drafting, web_search, memory, spreadsheet tools | Beginner | Large |
| 29 | **Climate & ESG Desk** | Impact investors, sustainability officers at SMBs, ESG-curious PMs | Monitors regulatory changes, tracks portfolio company ESG scores, summarises TCFD/SFDR developments, and flags greenwashing red flags | web_fetch (regulatory portals), web_search, memory, doc drafting, CDP/CDP public APIs | Advanced | Small |
| 30 | **Nutrition & Meal Planner** | Athletes, people with dietary restrictions, time-poor professionals wanting to eat well | Builds weekly meal plans from preferences and macros, generates shopping lists, tracks what's in the fridge, and adapts plans around schedule changes | web_search, recipe APIs (Spoonacular free tier), memory, calendar, list tools | Beginner | Mass market |

---

## Top 5 Highest-Potential Categories

### 1. 🏆 Personal Finance (#19) — *Mass market, low friction, high stickiness*
Every salaried adult in the developed world has a money management problem. The agent advantage is clear: unlike an app, a Personal Finance agent holds longitudinal context (knows your salary, your habits, your goals, your last conversation about the mortgage), reasons across it, and delivers a personalised weekly picture without the user having to enter anything manually beyond a CSV export. The install base is theoretically every person with a bank account. Monetisation is clean — subscription, no enterprise dependency.

### 2. 🥈 Job Hunt Ops (#13) — *High urgency, high willingness to pay*
Job hunting is acutely painful: fragmented across spreadsheets, portals, email threads, and memory. An agent that tracks every application, tailors CVs, preps interview briefs, and manages follow-ups is genuinely life-changing for the 6–18 months someone is in the market. Users are highly motivated (career anxiety = conversion) and will pay. Unlike a static tool, the agent's value compounds with memory of what's worked across applications.

### 3. 🥉 Real Estate Scout (#12) — *High-value decisions, deeply underserved by existing tools*
Property portals give you listings. They don't track what you've viewed, remember your compromises, monitor your saved search for price drops, or help you think through a decision. An agent does all of this and persists across a 6–24 month buying journey. Given property transaction values (£300k+), even a modest subscription fee is trivially justified. The agent unlocks genuine analytical depth (yield calcs, comparable analysis, neighbourhood trends) that Rightmove cannot.

### 4. 🔥 Travel Planner (#16) — *Mass adoption, network effects via memory*
Travel planning is genuinely time-consuming and the market is enormous. The agent advantage over Google Flights or TripAdvisor is total context: it knows you hate budget airlines, always want an aisle seat, prefer boutique hotels, have a shellfish allergy, and are travelling with a 4-year-old. Every trip gets better. Viral adoption potential is high — people share itineraries. Low technical barrier means fast time-to-value.

### 5. 💡 Competitive Intel (#26) — *SMB sweet spot, underserved by enterprise tools*
Enterprise teams have Crayon and Klue. Everyone else has nothing. Founders, PMs, and small strategy teams desperately need competitive awareness but can't afford £20k/year tools. An agent that scrapes, synthesises, and delivers a weekly battle card — and remembers what changed since last week — is a compelling, defensible package. It justifies a meaningful subscription price (this is a business tool) and solves a real, recurring pain point.
